landing.title: June - Product & Customer Analytics Software for B2B SaaS
landing.text:
June - Product & Customer Analytics Software for B2B SaaS
Product
Salesforce integration
NEW
HubSpot integration
Attio integration
Customers
Pricing
Content
Blogs
Guides
Changelog
A new chapter for June
August 8th, 2025
When we started June, we wanted to build a product that would be warm and welcoming, in a space that's often cold and intimidating.
From day one,
our ambition
, was to build a product analytics tool that can take you from idea to IPO.
Over the past 5 years we've gone from zero to profitable, which is a huge achievement. That said, our goal with June was to go big and break-out, not to stay small and break-even.
What we learned is that in an existing category, it's hard to start from the bottom end of the market and grow into an enterprise product without extensive funding.
This left us with two options:
Shift our focus to larger companies (moving away from our vision) to improve our growth rates and raise more funding
Optimise for smaller businesses, cutting any corner we can to make as much money as possible, like a budget airline
None of it felt right.
After seeing how our friends at
Command AI
joined Amplitude and integrated their product in just a few weeks, we realised that there was a third way:
"If you can't beat them, join them"
As a public company, Amplitude has all the resources to provide great service for both big and small businesses.
While it’s painful to say goodbye to the June product, this move gives us the opportunity to make a difference for companies of all sizes, from idea to IPO.
The team is serious about making their product the best. A year ago they launched their
Amplitude Made Easy
initiative, which was partly inspired by June.
They’re now giving us the opportunity to bring more of our best ideas into their product.
To our customers
We're proud of the work we've done and the relationships we've built over the years.
We've fortunate enough to personally onboard more than 1,000 startups to June over the years.
We hope to have helped you in your journey to build out your vision.
We’re especially grateful to the hundreds of you who have decided to join us at Amplitude, where we’ll continue to support you in your journey.
To our investors and supporters
From the early days, when Ferruccio and Enzo left Intercom to build June, we've been fortunate enough to find some early believers to back us.
As we move forward to a new chapter we wanted to thank everyone who has been part of our journey.
We want to thank
Frederik Hagenauer
from
Speedinvest
, the first investor in June, for his support and belief in our vision. And for always picking up the phone to help over the years as a Board Observer.
Y Combinator also took a chance on us admitting us to the Winter 2021 batch, and we're eternally grateful to our partners, especially
Gustaf Alströmer
and
Michael Seibel
for helping us navigate the chaos of our early days.
Louis Coppey
from
Point Nine Capital
, who joined our board after leading our seed round at the end of our YC batch, for teaching us how to run a board meeting, pushing us to be better, always taking the time to speak with candidates we hoped to hire, and supporting us whenever we needed it.
And to every angel who backed us, your belief shaped what June became.
We’re proud of what we built, but even more proud of the community around it. The heart of June isn’t in the code, it’s in the people who used it, shaped it, and believed in it.
We're grateful for the past and ready for the future.
The June Team
June is joining Amplitude
After 5 years of building June, we're joining Amplitude to continue our mission of helping companies build better products.

pricing.title: June - Pricing Plans
pricing.text:
June - Pricing Plans
Product
Salesforce integration
NEW
HubSpot integration
Attio integration
Customers
Pricing
Content
Blogs
Guides
Changelog
Let's retain your customers
We're a team of product people who will work with you to get the perfect setup for your company
All-in-one
Everything you need as a growing B2B SaaS company
Company-level Analytics
Product Analytics
Salesforce, HubSpot and Attio integrations
Custom insights with SQL & AI
Computed traits
1:1 support and onboarding
Company and audience alerts
Embed API
Start free trial
Trusted by next-gen B2B SaaS
Frequently asked questions
Is June right for me?
If preventing even a single customer from churning saves you $50K, then it could be a great fit
Do I really need it?
If you have a large user base but make only a small amount per user, or if your product is more 'set-and-forget', then another solution will be much better
Will you help me to set it up?
We’ll do whatever it takes to help you fully understand your customers’ usage while minimizing the need for your engineering resources. Setup fees vary based on implementation complexity
Do you have a startup plan?
No. June is a great fit for companies with at least $1M of ARR. If you’re earlier in your startup journey, you might be better off with a cheaper solution
"We started using June when one of our biggest customers churned at surprise. After that we sat down with the June team and worked on a great dashboard and workflows. It didn't happen anymore"
Timon Ruban
Co-founder of Luminovo
"One of the best things about June is that it is a platform that can be used by our entire team, not just the product manager. This has enabled us to get everyone on board with our data-driven approach, and ensures that everyone has access to the insights they need to do their job effectively"
Daniel Zarick
CEO of Arrows
June is joining Amplitude
After 5 years of building June, we're joining Amplitude to continue our mission of helping companies build better products.

hits.title: Apollo will close down on June 30th
ICQ will stop working from June 26
FCC will require phone carriers to authenticate calls by June 2021 [pdf]
Internet Explorer 11 (IE11) to be retired on June 15, 2022
Google Kubernetes Engine is introducing a cluster management fee on June 6
Google Photos will end its free unlimited storage in June 2021

analysis.name: June
analysis.tagline: Product & Customer Analytics Software for B2B SaaS
analysis.value_proposition: All-in-one product and customer analytics for B2B SaaS companies to prevent churn and understand usage across the full team.
analysis.ideal_customer: B2B SaaS companies with at least $1M ARR who need company-level retention analytics accessible to the whole team, not just product managers

features.name: company-level analytics
features.description: Analytics grouped and reported at the company/account level, not just individual users

features.name: product analytics
features.description: Core product usage analytics — event tracking, user behavior, funnels

features.name: salesforce integration
features.description: Bi-directional sync with Salesforce CRM

features.name: hubspot integration
features.description: Integration with HubSpot CRM

features.name: attio integration
features.description: Integration with Attio CRM

features.name: sql custom insights
features.description: Custom analytics queries written in SQL for bespoke analysis

features.name: ai custom insights
features.description: AI-assisted custom analytics and insight generation

features.name: computed traits
features.description: Derived user/company properties computed from event data

features.name: company and audience alerts
features.description: Automated alerts triggered by company-level or segment-level behavioral thresholds

features.name: embed api
features.description: API for embedding June analytics views or data into external surfaces

features.name: 1:1 onboarding and support
features.description: Hands-on setup assistance and ongoing 1:1 support from the June team

features.name: browser sdk
features.description: Client-side JavaScript SDK for tracking events in the browser

features.name: react / next.js sdk
features.description: First-class SDK for React and Next.js applications

features.name: node.js sdk
features.description: Server-side SDK for Node.js backends

features.name: python sdk
features.description: Server-side SDK for Python backends

features.name: ruby sdk
features.description: Server-side SDK for Ruby backends

features.name: http api
features.description: Language-agnostic HTTP API for tracking events, users, and groups

features.name: user identification
features.description: Identify and associate anonymous events to named users

features.name: group / account tracking
features.description: Associate users to companies/accounts and track group-level behavior

features.name: event tracking
features.description: Custom event capture for user and group actions

features.name: page tracking
features.description: Automatic or manual page view tracking

features.name: churn monitoring dashboards
features.description: Dashboards and workflows designed to surface at-risk customers before they churn
features.evidence_quote: We started using June when one of our biggest customers churned at surprise... It didn't happen anymore

features.name: soc 2 type ii compliance
features.description: SOC 2 Type II security certification

features.name: gdpr compliance
features.description: GDPR-compliant data handling

differentiators: Company-level (account-level) analytics purpose-built for B2B SaaS, not just individual user tracking
Designed for whole-team use, not just product managers — accessible to non-technical stakeholders
Hands-on 1:1 onboarding and support included, minimizing engineering resources required
Positioned explicitly for retention and churn prevention, not just growth metrics

recent_ships.what: June acquired by / joined Amplitude; product being sunset and team moving to Amplitude
recent_ships.source: After 5 years of building June, we're joining Amplitude to continue our mission of helping companies build better products.

recent_ships.what: Salesforce integration launched (marked NEW on site)

praised: Helped prevent customer churn surprises — 'We started using June when one of our biggest customers churned at surprise... It didn't happen anymore' (Timon Ruban, Luminovo)
Accessible to entire team, not just product manager — 'a platform that can be used by our entire team, not just the product manager' (Daniel Zarick, Arrows CEO)
Enables data-driven culture across the org — 'ensures that everyone has access to the insights they need to do their job effectively'
sources: g2 (rating only, no review text retrieved)

tiers.name: All-in-one
tiers.headline_inclusions: Company analytics, product analytics, CRM integrations, SQL/AI insights, computed traits, alerts, embed API, 1:1 support

pricing.notes: No public pricing shown on pricing page — 'Start free trial' CTA with no listed price. FAQ states June requires at least $1M ARR and is not suitable for early-stage startups. Setup fees mentioned as variable by implementation complexity. Product is being sunset following August 2025 acquisition by Amplitude; pricing likely no longer actively maintained.
